resources.title: House rules agents can actually follow
resources.summary: The recurring repo rules: cleanup, stack, schemas, IDs, and CI, written as constraints an agent can check.

resources.title: AGENTS.md as the house-style file
resources.summary: One place for toolchain, code style, and repo invariants, with every other agent note pointing back to it.

resources.title: Add Discord notifications without breaking Slack
resources.summary: Find every Slack notification path, add the Discord twin, and keep the old channel working.

resources.title: Fix the whole pattern, not one instance
resources.summary: Find every occurrence, patch the full surface, and write the rule down so the drift does not come back.

resources.title: Read-only audit with file:line findings
resources.summary: A strict no-write pass that reports one concrete finding per line, sorted by impact.

resources.title: Commit, push, and open the PR cleanly
resources.summary: Branch when needed, make one conventional commit, keep the PR scoped, and report the exact SHA.

resources.title: Final cleanup pass before calling it done
resources.summary: Re-scan the whole project for leftovers, stale docs, missed simplifications, and rule violations.

resources.title: Plan before touching the code
resources.summary: For large or ambiguous work: inspect first, write the steps, ask the blocking questions, then execute.

resources.title: End-of-task reports that are useful later
resources.summary: A short closeout with changed files, commands and results, decisions made, and honest follow-up.

resources.title: Security review of the actual diff
resources.summary: A read-only pass over dependency bumps, auth changes, and CI edits, with a fixed checklist and findings format.

resources.title: Verification gate before done
resources.summary: Run typecheck, lint, tests, API flow, and build; keep fixing until the repo agrees.

resources.title: Commit and PR text from the real diff
resources.summary: A conventional commit subject and PR body that say what changed, why it matters, and how it was checked.

resources.title: Dependency updates that only land green
resources.summary: A scheduled job bumps packages, runs the repo checks, and pushes only when the branch earns it.

resources.title: design.md for keeping generated UI on-system
resources.summary: The short token and component contract that stops agent-built UI from drifting into visual noise.

resources.title: Domain and TLS expiry watcher
resources.summary: A daily check that ranks domains and certificates by expiry, then keeps one rolling issue current.

resources.title: Incident timelines while evidence is fresh
resources.summary: An agent follows logs and metrics during an incident so the timeline is accurate before context fades.

resources.title: Gate production migrations before review
resources.summary: Block locking DDL, unsafe deploy order, and unbatched backfills before a human has to catch them.

resources.title: How to Structure Coding-Agent Instructions in a Large Codebase
resources.summary: A practical layout for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, Copilot instructions, scoped folder rules, and docs when several agents work in the same repo.

resources.title: Teach agents to read EXPLAIN
resources.summary: Give it the plan, get back the slow node, estimate drift, and the index worth testing.

resources.title: Postmortems from logs, not memory
resources.summary: Turn the incident channel and system evidence into a timeline that names systems, never people.

resources.title: Remove trivial tests before they harden
resources.summary: An agent skill for replacing shallow AI-written coverage with tests that can catch real regressions.

resources.title: Staff-engineer review with a narrow charter
resources.summary: A terse review prompt for correctness, retry-safety, blast radius, and the smallest useful fix.

resources.title: Compress a worklog without losing signal
resources.summary: Turn messy session notes into a dense developer journal while preserving decisions, commands, and evidence.

resources.title: DaisyUI and Tailwind without visual drift
resources.summary: Use the real components, match the reference theme, and verify every page in light and dark.

resources.title: Docs and diagrams before the work is done
resources.summary: Update the README, comments, Mermaid flows, and AGENTS.md decisions while the context is still fresh.

resources.title: Prove parity during a refactor
resources.summary: Capture reference behavior, separate public from internal fields, and make the new path match what matters.

resources.title: Observability that makes failures inspectable
resources.summary: Traceable logs, level control, test ids where they help, and no silent failure paths.

resources.title: Parallel agents with clean write scopes
resources.summary: Plan first, split the work into disjoint surfaces, fan out, then reconcile and verify the whole result.

resources.title: PHP to Python with golden-file parity
resources.summary: Move behavior across languages with fixture proof, uv tooling, and boring first-party libraries where they fit.

resources.title: Root-cause investigation before edits
resources.summary: Find the exact source with file:line, name the systemic cause, and avoid masking symptoms with a patch.

resources.title: UI verification with real browser evidence
resources.summary: Run user and guest flows, include hard refreshes, and keep screenshots as proof of what changed.

resources.title: Write tests that can actually fail
resources.summary: Behavior checks, negative cases, regression coverage, and cleanup that keeps the next run honest.

resources.title: Spec, scaffold, then tests
resources.summary: Write the design first, let the agent scaffold the shape and failing tests, then fill in the logic.
